rubric_items.reasoning: Correct, durable approach: StatefulSet scale-out (which recreates PVCs via existing volumeClaimTemplates) followed by rs.add() of the two new members via their stable headless-service DNS names. This is the standard, restart-safe way to grow a non-operator replica set. Verified via rs.status() (1 PRIMARY + 4 SECONDARY, health=1), rs.conf().members.length==5, original document intact, and a fresh write/read succeeding. Minor ding: both new members were added back-to-back without confirming each completed initial sync before adding the next, and only a fixed 15s sleep was used to assert health rather than explicitly checking sync state (STARTUP2) — harmless here given the trivial dataset, but not fully rigorous.

Direct, logical sequence: confirm new pods/PVCs are up post-scale, add both replica members, briefly wait, check rs.status(), verify pre-existing data, then do a fresh write/read and confirm member count. No backtracking or trial-and-error visible in the log.

Used targeted, least-privilege commands throughout: scoped kubectl get/exec calls against the mongodb namespace, and mongosh eval for replica-set administration (rs.add, rs.status, rs.conf) — exactly the tools appropriate for this task with no broad or risky operations.

Minimal, focused change: one scale mutation plus the two required rs.add() calls, with only read/verification commands otherwise (23 total calls, 1 mutation). No collateral changes or unnecessary resources touched.
